heading: Incentives for transfer to qualified purchasers
label: 4110 Incentives for transfer to qualified purchasers

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: With respect to any eligible low-income housing for which an owner has submitted a second notice of intent under to transfer the housing to a qualified purchaser, the owner shall offer the housing for transfer to qualified purchasers as provided in this section. The Secretary shall issue regulations describing the means by which potential qualified purchasers shall be notified of the availability of the housing for sale. The Secretary shall take into account the Federal cost limits under for the housing when providing incentives under section 4109(b)(2) and (b)(3) of this title (pursuant to subsection (d)(3) of this section).section 4106(d) of this titlesection 4105(a) of this title

children.heading: Right of first offer to priority purchasers
children.label: b Right of first offer to priority purchasers

children.heading: Negotiation period
children.label: 1 Negotiation period
children.text: For the 12-month period beginning on the receipt by the Secretary of a second notice of intent under with respect to such housing, the owner may offer to sell and negotiate a sale of the housing only with priority purchasers. The negotiated sale price may not exceed the preservation value of the housing determined under . The owner or the purchaser shall submit a plan of action under for any sale under this subsection, which shall include any request for assistance under this section, upon the acceptance of any bona fide offer meeting the requirements of this paragraph.section 4106(d) of this titlesection 4103(b)(2) of this titlesection 4107 of this title

children.heading: Expression of interest
children.label: 2 Expression of interest
children.text: During such period, priority purchasers may submit written notice to the Secretary stating their interest in acquiring the housing. Such notice shall be made in the form and include such information as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Information
children.label: 3 Information
children.text: Within 30 days of receipt of an expression of interest by a priority purchaser, the Secretary shall provide such purchaser with information on the assistance available from the Federal Government to facilitate a transfer and the owner shall provide appropriate information on the housing, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Right of refusal for other qualified purchasers
children.label: c Right of refusal for other qualified purchasers
children.text: If no bona fide offer to purchase any eligible low-income housing subject to this section that meets the requirements of subsection (b) is made and accepted during the period under such subsection, during the 3-month period beginning upon the expiration of the 12-month period under subsection (b)(1), the owner of the housing may offer to sell and may sell the housing only to qualified purchasers. The negotiated sale price may not exceed the preservation value of the housing determined under . The owner or purchaser shall submit a plan of action under for any sale under this subsection, which shall include any request for assistance under this section, upon the acceptance of any bona fide offer meeting the requirements of this paragraph.section 4103(b)(2) of this titlesection 4107 of this title1So in original. Probably should be “subsection.”1

children.heading: Approval
children.label: 1 Approval
children.text: If the qualified purchaser is a resident council, the Secretary may not approve a plan of action for assistance under this section unless the council’s proposed resident homeownership program meets the requirements under . For all other qualified purchasers, the Secretary may not approve the plan unless the Secretary finds that the criteria for approval under have been satisfied.section 4116 of this titlesection 4112 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: acquire the eligible low-income housing from the current owner for a purchase price not greater than the preservation equity of the housing;

children.label: B
children.text: pay the debt service on the federally-assisted mortgage covering the housing;

children.label: C
children.text: pay the debt service on any loan for the rehabilitation of the housing;

children.label: D
children.text: meet project operating expenses and establish adequate reserves for the housing, and in the case of a priority purchaser, meet project oversight costs;

children.label: E
children.text: receive a distribution equal to an 8 percent annual return on any actual cash investment (from sources other than assistance provided under this title ) made to acquire or rehabilitate the project;2See References in Text note below.2

children.label: F
children.text: in the case of a priority purchaser, receive a reimbursement of all reasonable transaction expenses associated with the acquisition, loan closing, and implementation of an approved plan of action; and

children.label: G
children.text: in the case of an approved resident homeownership program, cover the costs of training for the resident council, homeownership counseling and training, the fees for the nonprofit entity or public agency working with the resident council and costs related to relocation of tenants who elect to move.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For all qualified purchasers of housing under this subsection, the Secretary may provide assistance for an approved plan of action in the form of 1 or more of the incentives authorized under , except that the incentive under such may include an acquisition loan under section 1715z–6(f) of this title.section 4109(b) of this titlesection 4109(b)(7) of this title2

children.heading: Priority purchasers
children.label: B Priority purchasers
children.text: Where the qualified purchaser is a priority purchaser, the Secretary may provide assistance for an approved plan of action (in the form of a grant) for each unit in the housing in an amount, as determined by the Secretary, that does not exceed the present value of the total of the projected published fair market rentals for existing housing (established by the Secretary under ) for the next 10 years (or such longer period if additional assistance is necessary to cover the costs referred to in paragraph (2)).section 1437f(c) of title 42
